description: A grammatical Phi quotation opened by 'shola' (QUOT.COMP) reaches its boundary at 'sholo'. The surrounding clause then resumes with a verb that says whether those words were spoken or heard.
articulatory_notes: Start with grooved unvoiced 'sh' in the postalveolar region and move into mid-back rounded 'o'. At 'l', the tongue point settles against the gumline above the teeth; continue lateral voicing and keep the same lip rounding through final 'o'. Accent 'sho'.
usage_notes: 'haolu', 'shemui', and 'thilou' report spoken words; 'hea' reports words received by hearing. Slot 1 marking stands between 'sholo' and that verb where it normally belongs. When quotations nest, this closer matches the nearest open 'shola'.

examples.phi: mia shola thia ha nai sholo to hea.
